---
title: "LegalIA"
subtitle: "Computational analysis of Mexico's Diario Oficial de la Federación"
toc: false
number-sections: false
---

The *Diario Oficial de la Federación* (DOF) is the official gazette of the
Mexican State: its pages carry the laws, decrees, agreements, resolutions and
notices that give legal form to the country's public life. Each edition is
composed of *notas* — individual documentary units, each with its own title,
section and issuing body — and that daily flow, sustained for more than a
century, constitutes one of the most complete records of the activity of the
Mexican State.

**LegalIA** is a project by [INGEOTEC](https://github.com/INGEOTEC) that turns
that record into an object of computational study. The project gathers, in a
single repository, the tools to download the DOF and transform it into formats
suitable for natural language processing, together with a series of
explorations that document, with quantitative methods, what the archive
contains and how it has changed over time. The gazette is written in Spanish,
and so are the titles and terms quoted throughout the analyses; the prose that
examines them is in English.

## The corpus {.unnumbered}

The starting point is the DOF's note archive, obtained from the gazette's own
open-data service. The collection covers, without interruption, from 1970 to
the present and includes, for every publication day, the metadata of all the
notes in its morning, evening and extraordinary editions. To date the archive
holds close to one million notes spread over more than fourteen thousand
publication days. The analyses on this site are built on that metadata and, in
later stages, on the full text of the notes.

## The explorations {.unnumbered}

Each exploration is a reproducible document: the text, figures and tables are
generated from aggregates derived from the archive, and the code that produces
them accompanies every page. The pages are written as Jupyter notebooks, so
they can also be opened and executed outside the site — for instance on
[Google Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/). The first installment
examines the titles of the notes:

- [The titles of the Diario Oficial, 1970--2026](explorations/titles.ipynb). A
  quantitative reading of the gazette's most elementary metadata: how many
  notes are published, how they are named, which documentary types dominate
  each era, and what their vocabulary reveals about the priorities of the
  State.

## The tools {.unnumbered}

The repository is organized as a monorepo of Python packages, each installable
and versioned independently:
[dof2md](https://github.com/INGEOTEC/LegalIA/tree/master/packages/dof2md)
downloads complete DOF editions as PDF and converts them to Markdown;
[dofjson](https://github.com/INGEOTEC/LegalIA/tree/master/packages/dofjson) is
a client for the DOF's open-data JSON service; and
[nota2md](https://github.com/INGEOTEC/LegalIA/tree/master/packages/nota2md)
builds the Markdown of an individual note from its HTML content or by OCR of
its scanned page images.
